theater_description: Eastern Province campaign beginning with Mavil Aru and extending through Muttur, Sampur, Vakarai and Thoppigala.
geometry_method_note: Province-and-corridor level footprint; fighting shifted among eastern fronts and district clusters rather than staying in a single pocket.
military_area_note: Only eastern-operating Sri Lankan formations and LTTE eastern units would belong in the denominator; national-force size would overstate exposure.
civilian_area_note: Civilian denominator should be restricted to affected eastern districts and especially high-exposure corridors such as Vakarai, not whole-country Sri Lanka.
split_reason: Different theater, different civilian footprint, and different displacement pattern from the later northern and final-pocket fighting.
quality_note: Proposed only; no full episode estimate completed in this bundle.

theater_description: Northern Province advance through Mannar, Vavuniya, Kilinochchi and western approaches into Mullaittivu before the first No Fire Zone.
geometry_method_note: Moving-front geometry across multiple districts; exposure footprint changed too much for a single clean denominator without a larger reconstruction project.
military_area_note: Relevant denominator is Wanni-operating divisions and attached brigades, not the whole Sri Lankan Army.
civilian_area_note: Civilian denominator must track repeated displacement within the Wanni rather than use the Northern Province total or national population.
split_reason: Front moved materially across districts; source coverage aligns differently from the compressed final-pocket phase.
quality_note: Proposed only; no full episode estimate completed in this bundle.

episode_name: First NFZ to early second NFZ, Mullaittivu data-anchored episode
theater_description: Compressed retreat corridor in Mullaittivu District spanning the first government No Fire Zone and the transition into the second No Fire Zone, plus adjacent combat area where civilians and LTTE forces remained intermingled.
geometry_method_note: Episode geometry is anchored to the 20 January-7 March 2009 UN RC/HC casualty note. The combat area reportedly shrank from about 100 km² to 45 km² during January-February, including a 14 km² NFZ by mid-February. First NFZ map from ICG is 35.5 km²; second NFZ 14 km².
military_area_note: Military denominator is restricted to directly operating Sri Lankan formations pressing the pocket and LTTE fighters still inside or immediately around the pocket. Whole-army national strength is rejected.
civilian_area_note: Civilian denominator uses civilians physically trapped in the Mullaittivu conflict area and NFZ/adjacent strip, adjusted for fast displacement and escape; whole-country or province-wide totals are rejected.
split_reason: Strongest source convergence for a bounded civilian numerator and displacement-sensitive civilian denominator occurs in this window.
quality_note: Civilian numerator and civilian denominator are much better sourced than the combatant numerator. DR remains provisional because combatant deaths are model-generated.

theater_description: Putumattalan-Valayanmadam coastal strip after the early second NFZ phase and before the mass escape beginning 20 April.
geometry_method_note: Very small and shifting coastal strip with continued shelling of hospitals and rapid civilian compression.
military_area_note: Only directly operating pocket-reduction formations belong in the denominator.
civilian_area_note: Civilian denominator changes rapidly due to escape attempts, deaths, and internal crowding; requires daily or weekly reconstruction.
split_reason: Theater compresses sharply and available counts become less cleanly separable than in the 20 January-7 March window.
quality_note: Proposed only. Later-source numerators increasingly mix civilians and LTTE cadres or rely on incomplete hospital/medical series.

episode_name: Final coastal strip and third NFZ
theater_description: Karayamullivaikkal-Vellamullivaikkal-Vadduvakal final pocket including the third No Fire Zone.
geometry_method_note: Micro-pocket shrank to a few square kilometres with intense final fighting and mass breakout.
military_area_note: Relevant denominator is the final assault force and remaining LTTE cadres, not the national military.
civilian_area_note: Civilian denominator must track the final trapped population and mass departures after 20 April.
split_reason: Final micro-pocket has qualitatively different geometry and the strongest mortality controversy, but also the greatest risk of mixed civilian/combatant counts.
quality_note: Proposed only. Final-phase civilian totals are heavily debated and many available series do not cleanly separate civilians from LTTE cadres.
